architecture.agent_shape: all five pools, zero named sessions
architecture.concurrency: multiple chains in flight simultaneously per rig (validated in Phase 4)

stories.title: gh-stats: list-prs --repo OWNER/REPO with json and table output
stories.compares_to_v1_3_story: gs-id4 (same body, replayed verbatim except for the v2.0-specific Notes section)
stories.note_no_pr: gh-stats has no GitHub remote; chain landed locally on the branch (final_state=branch_ready_no_pr)

worker.covers: load-context + plan + workspace-setup + implement + self-audit + submit

reviewer.note: session_id matches tester — pool-instance reuse on the second handoff; benign

finalizer.covers: rebase + close (no PR path — gh-stats has no remote)

v1_3_vs_v2_0_single_story.comparison_caveat: v2.0 story (gs-uud) was filed on a fresh bd init; v1.3 story (gs-id4) was filed on a populated bead store. Workspace-state differences are minor but not zero.
decomposition_notes: Worker phase in v2.0 (22m 17s) covers plan + workspace + implement + self-audit; v1.3's planner + implementor sum to 10m 32s (632s). v2.0 worker is ~12 min longer. self-audit (lint+types+rule audits) is new work; workspace-setup and per-step metadata writes also live inside the single session now, where v1.3 spread them across handoffs.
Tester phase shrank from 21s (v1.3) to 86s (v2.0) — but v1.3's tester only ran pytest, while v2.0's tester runs pytest + ruff + mypy in a fresh session that has to load context.
Reviewer phase grew from 3m 24s (v1.3) to 5m 41s (v2.0). Reviewer scope is similar; the longer time may be from richer rule self-audits the v2.0 prompt encourages, or from fresh-context loading.
Documenter phase grew from 3m 16s (v1.3) to 3m 51s (v2.0) — small delta, expected.
Finalizer phase is 51s (new in v2.0; no v1.3 equivalent because v1.3 documenter handled merge gate inline).

Inter-phase gaps grew because v2.0 has five handoffs (worker→tester→reviewer→documenter→finalizer) vs v1.3's four (planner→implementor→tester→reviewer→documenter). Per-handoff gap is roughly comparable; total gap time grows with one extra handoff.

concurrency_capability.v1_3: single chain at a time per rig (named sessions serialize)
concurrency_capability.v2_0: multiple chains in flight per rig, bounded by host CPU/RAM and per-account API rate limits

v1_3_vs_v2_0_single_story.ship_signal: On single-story wall clock alone, v1.3 is faster. The v2.0 win shows up only under concurrency — which Phase 4's 10-story test will measure. Single-story slowdown is the cost of architectural separation (more handoffs, more pool spawn latency, more fresh-context loading) that buys parallelism.

supervisor_overhead_observed.dolt_log_warning: every reconciler poll emits 'error running query: nothing to commit' — bd CLI wraps reads in write-capable transactions
supervisor_overhead_observed.impact_on_chain: none observed; chain progressed normally despite Dolt at 70-100% CPU
supervisor_overhead_observed.tracked_under: fit-and-finish todo (deep dive deferred until v2.0 ships)
